info.title: Disable Injection and Prevention Rules Public API
info.description:
The Disable Injection and Prevention Rules API lets you add and manage rules in Cortex XDR that define process-level exceptions, bypassing prevention modules and injection. These rules can be applied only to endpoints running Cortex XDR agent version 7.9 or later.    

This API allows you to:
- Fetch existing Disable Injection and Prevention rules with filtering and pagination.
- Add new Disable Injection and Prevention rules to generate a temporary exception to bypass a process from prevention modules and injections.
- Disable existing rules by their IDs.

You can export the OpenAPI specs in the [Cortex Stoplight instance](https://cortex-panw.stoplight.io/docs/cortex-xsiam-3-x/branches/main/9jldcj8imsddh-disable-injection-and-prevention-rules-public-api)

**Required license:** In Cortex XSIAM Premium, Cortex XSIAM Enterprise, or Cortex XSIAM NG SIEM, requires the Cortex Cloud Posture Management add-on.

post.summary: Get Disable Injection and Prevention rules
post.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of Disable Injection and Prevention rules based on optional filters and sorting criteria.

This endpoint allows you to:
- Retrieve all rules or filter by specific criteria
- Sort results by any field in ascending or descending order
- Paginate through large result sets
- Get total count and filtered count of rules

post.summary: Add Disable Injection and Prevention rule
post.description:
Creates a new Disable Injection and Prevention rule to allow specific processes to bypass injection prevention.

This endpoint allows you to:
- Define a new rule with a unique name and description
- Specify the target platform (Windows, Linux, or macOS)
- Set the process name and path to be protected
- Configure expiration time
- Apply the rule globally or to specific profiles

request_data.description: Allow injection for trusted Windows application across all endpoints
Allow injection for trusted Windows application across all endpoints

post.summary: Disable Disable Injection and Prevention Rules
post.description:
Disables one or more existing Disable Injection and Prevention rules by their IDs.

This endpoint allows you to:
- Disable multiple rules in a single request
- Immediately stop rules from being applied

GetDataRequest.description: Request body containing pagination, sorting, and filtering parameters.

search_from.description: Starting index for pagination (zero-based). Defines the offset from which to begin returning results.

search_to.description: Ending index for pagination (exclusive). Defines the maximum number of results to return (search_to - search_from).

field.description: The field name to filter on. For example, `rule_id`, `rule_name`, `status`,`platform`.

SortingRequest.description: Defines sorting criteria for query results.

FilterRequest.description: Define filter condition for querying data.

field.description: The field name to filter on. For example, `rule_id`, `rule_name`, `status`,`platform`.

operator.description: Comparison operator to use for filtering. For example, (`eq` (equals), `neq` (not equals), `gte` (greater than or equal), `lte` (less than or equal), `contains` or `not_contains`)

value.description:
The value to compare against. Type should match the field type.
Can be a string or number depending on the operator.
**Examples:**
* If the `field` is `status`, the `value` can be `active`, `disabled`, or `expired`
* If the `field` is `platform`, the `value` can be a string such as `windows`, `macos`, or `linux`

data.description: Disable injection prevention for critical business application

reply.description: Container object for the response data and metadata

filter_count.description: The number of rules returned in the current response after applying filters.

total_count.description: The total number of rules available that match the filter criteria, regardless of pagination.

reply.description: Returns the Disable Injection and Prevention Rule ID.

DisableInjectionRulePAPIObject.description: Array of Disable Injection and Prevention rule objects matching the query criteria.

rule_id.description: Unique identifier for the rule. Auto-generated upon rule creation and used for all subsequent operations.

description.description: Description of the rule's purpose and context.
description.example: Disable injection prevention for critical business application

platform.description: Target operating system to which the rule applies. For example: `windows`, `linux`, or `macos`.

status.description:
Current status of the rule. For example: `active`, `disabled`, or `expired`.
- `active`: Rule is active and being applied
- `disabled`: Rule is inactive and not being applied
- `expired`: Rule has passed its expiration time

expiration_time.description: Unix timestamp (milliseconds) when the rule will expire.

process_name.description: Name of the process executable to which this rule applies. Should match the exact process name as it appears in the system.

path.description: Full file system path to the process executable.

created_by.description: Name of the user or API key ID that created this rule.

creation_time.description: Unix timestamp (milliseconds) when the rule was created.

is_global.description:
Indicates whether the rule applies globally to all endpoints or only to specific profiles.
- `true`: Rule applies to all endpoints in the tenant.
- `false`: Rule applies only to endpoints in the specified `profile_ids`.

profile_ids.description:
Array of profile IDs to which this rule applies. Only relevant when is_global is false. 
`null` or empty when `is_global` is `true`.

DisableInjectionAddRulePAPIRequest.description: Request schema for creating a new Disable Injection and Prevention rule. All required fields must be provided to successfully create a rule.

rule_name.description: Unique name for the new rule. Must be between 1-100 characters and should be descriptive of the rule's purpose.

description.example: Allow injection for trusted Windows application across all endpoints
description.description: Description explaining the purpose and context of this rule. Maximum 500 characters.

platform.description: Target operating system to which the rule applies.

process_name.description: Name of the process executable to protect. Must match the exact process name (1-100 characters).

path.description: Full file system path to the process executable. Must be a valid path for the specified platform (1-1000 characters).

hours_to_expiration.description: Number of hours until the rule expires. After expiration, the rule will no longer be applied. For example, 24 hours.

profile_ids.description:
Array of profile IDs to which this rule should apply. 
Required when `scope` is `profile`. Must be `null` or omitted when scope is `global`.

scope.description:
Defines the application scope of the rule.
- `global`: Rule applies to all endpoints all matching endpoints (by platform and agent version >= 7.9)
- `profile`: Rule applies only to endpoints in the specified `profile_ids`

DisableInjectionDisableRulesPAPIRequest.description: Request schema for disabling one or more existing Disable Injection Prevention rules. Requires at least one valid rule ID.

rule_ids.description: Array of rule IDs to disable. Must contain at least one valid rule ID.
